text: Code refactoring is the process of improving the structure of existing code without changing its behavior. Think of it like cleaning a messy room—you don't buy new furniture; you just organize what you already have. For programmers, this means renaming unclear variables, breaking long functions into smaller parts, or removing duplicate code. The goal is to make the code easier to read and understand. Good refactoring also reduces a problem called "technical debt." This is like the extra work you create when you take shortcuts. By regularly refactoring, you keep your code clean and avoid bigger headaches later.
